description: This file is an approved high-level context pack for selected larger projects Jeremiah has worked on. It is intended for AI prompt grounding when users ask about project architecture, responsibilities, and implementation approach. Treat it as summary context, avoid guessing beyond these points, and acknowledge when requested details are not explicitly documented here.

sections.heading: Jeremiah.dev project
points: jeremiah.dev is a pnpm workspace monorepo centered on a portfolio experience and an AI assistant experience.
The assistant stack includes topic inference, approved profile documents, and server-side OpenAI request handling behind validation and abuse protections.
The architecture separates prompt/control logic from UI rendering so profile context and routing can evolve without rewriting the chat interface.
The portfolio side and AI side share monorepo workflows and scripts, allowing consistent build, lint, and deployment habits across apps.

sections.heading: Sitation project
points: Sitation focuses on attaching contextual comments to live webpages through a companion extension and web application.
Its repository structure separates a Next.js app, extension targets, shared contracts, and database concerns so browser and server boundaries stay explicit.
Data shape and validation responsibilities are shared through package-level contracts to keep extension and backend behavior aligned.
The project workflow uses monorepo tooling for multi-package builds, typing, and release preparation.

sections.heading: Vestry project
points: Vestry is organized as a workspace monorepo with turbo-based task orchestration and shared package patterns.
The project follows a component-library-first approach, with reusable UI and config layers intended to support multiple applications.
Build scripts and workspace conventions are designed to standardize code quality and accelerate cross-app iteration.
Its structure is suitable for scaling design and utility packages alongside product-facing app surfaces.

sections.heading: Circledating project
points: Circledating is represented as an unreleased product and documentation mirror for a relationship-focused platform concept.
The implementation is described with a Next.js stack using TypeScript, utility-first styling, and authentication/data tooling.
Architecture signals suggest account-driven workflows, profile or community interactions, and persistence via Prisma-based models.
The repository context should be treated as product and technical framing rather than a full public implementation.

sections.heading: Ozark Highlands Shepherds project
points: Ozark Highlands Shepherds is a Nuxt 3 site supporting a content-rich public presence for the kennel.
The app pairs Nuxt rendering with Tailwind styling and CMS-backed content flows via Sanity tooling.
Project organization follows common Nuxt boundaries including pages, components, layouts, and server entry points.
Content operations are supported with schema and import tooling to maintain editable structured site data.

sections.heading: Oreillyauto.com project
points: Oreillyauto.com work includes enterprise e-commerce front-end delivery in a multi-team environment.
Responsibilities included technical leadership, implementation guidance, and shipping production-facing features across the software delivery lifecycle.
The approved career context ties this work to collaboration-heavy engineering practices such as code review, mentoring, testing, and process improvement.
Technology context associated with this work includes React/TypeScript-centric web development plus supporting platform tooling.

sections.heading: O'Reilly Component Library project
points: This project refers to a component library Jeremiah built for O'Reilly use cases spanning React and React Native surfaces.
The library context is design-system oriented: reusable UI primitives, consistent interaction patterns, and shared implementation standards.
A core goal is reducing duplication across teams by providing stable, composable components with predictable usage patterns.
When discussing this project, frame it as a shared UI foundation used to improve consistency, delivery speed, and maintainability.
